item.title: REGEX
item.description: ================================== What is it A tool to match strings When You want to check the format of a string that a user enters Complete In the right format Its about pattern matching Syntax \w = word characters -not punctuation \won will match don, ron, ton, \W = only punctuation, no characters  \wing\W\w\W\wing = ring-a-ding \d = 0 through 9 \D = non-digits . = any `[aeiou] = match any from a set `[a-z] = match one or more from a set `[a-z,A-Z] = match in multiple ranges \ = escape character Example path match: `\\\w+(\\w+)+

item.title: Package installation
item.description: Version Warning make sure you are using version 2.7.2 or higher otherwise PFSense will be unable to locate the registry. go to package manager click on install packages select the package you want to install

item.title: Show commands
item.description: Show flash -shows the flash mem

item.title: OSPFv2-Concept
item.description:
a large area dynamic routing protocol. Elect a Designated Router (DR) Elect Backup Designated Router (BDR) All other routers are Drothers highest score wins order of importance Interface Priority Router ID Loopback address Active IP address on any interface on the router NOTE: Interface Priority We don’t change the interface priority very often. the DR is responsible for collecting and distributing all of the Link State Advertisements (LSAs) within the area. it stores these in a database which it then is responsible for sending to all of the Drothers. the DR uses the multicast address 224.0.0.5 for all OSPF routers. the BDR maintains a close relationship with the DR and keeps a backup of all relationships and the database. in the event that the DR is no longer active it assumes the role of DR. its a read only version of the Link State Database all routers or layer 3 switches in an area that are neither a DR or a BDR. use the multicast address 224.0.0.6 to send messages to the DR and BDR also see WCM - Wild Card Mask
OSPFv2-example
layer 3 Switch OSPF v2 example

item.title: Circuit Equations
item.description: Rt = total resistance R1 = resistance of resistor 1 R1 = resistance of resistor 2 so on and so fourth Rt = total resistance R1 = resistance of resistor 1 R1 = resistance of resistor 2 so on and so fourth

OSPFv2-example
NAT and PAT-example
Switchports on a Router - config

RIP2 - config
HSRP Example

item.title: HSRP or FSRP
item.description:
RT01 --active device En Conf t Int `[HSRP int] !Standby --represents HSRP configs Standby version 2 Standby [group] ip [DG address] Standby [group] priority [priority] Standby [group] preempt --elections when a router goes down and comes back RT020 En Conf t Int [HSRP int] Standby version 2 Standby [group] ip [DG address] Standby [group] preempt also see HSRP Example
HSRP - switches Example

item.title: Switching configs
item.description:
VLANs with a trunk (ROAS)
HSRP - switches Example

item.title: HSRP - switches Example
item.description:
en conf t hostname RF-137-L10-SW01 vlan 10 name Production-Data vlan 15 name Backup-Data vlan 99 name NATIVE vlan 999 name BLACK-HOLE exit int vlan 10 ip address 172.16.10.251 255.255.255.0 no shut exit int vlan 15 ip address 172.16.15.250 255.255.255.0 no shut exit int f0/24 switchport mode trunk switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,15 switchport trunk native vlan 99 no shut exit int f0/23 switchport mode trunk switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,15 switchport trunk native vlan 99 no shut exit int range f0/1-22 switchport mode access switchport access vlan 10 no shut exit en conf t hostname RF-137-L10-SW02 vlan 10 name Production-Data vlan 15 name Backup-Data vlan 99 name NATIVE vlan 999 name BLACK-HOLE exit int vlan 15 ip address 172.16.15.251 255.255.255.0 no shut exit int vlan 10 ip address 172.16.10.250 255.255.255.0 no shut exit int f0/24 switchport mode trunk switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,15 switchport trunk native vlan 99 no shut exit int f0/23 switchport mode trunk switchport trunk allowed vlan 10,15 switchport trunk native vlan 99 no shut exit also see HSRP Example
HSRP or FSRP

item.title: HSRP Example
item.description: en conf t hostname RF-137-L10-RT01 int g0/0 no shut exit int g0/0.10 encapsulation dot1q 10 ip address 172.16.10.253 255.255.255.0 standby version 2 standby 1 ip 172.16.10.254 standby 1 priority 255 standby 1 preempt exit int g0/0.15 encapsulation dot1q 15 ip address 172.16.15.252 255.255.255.0 standby version 2 standby 2 ip 172.16.15.254 exit en conf t hostname RF-137-L10-RT02 int g0/0 no shut exit int g0/0.15 encapsulation dot1q 15 ip address 172.16.15.253 255.255.255.0 standby version 2 standby 2 ip 172.16.15.254 standby 2 priority 255 standby 2 preempt exit int g0/0.10 encapsulation dot1q 10 ip address 172.16.10.252 255.255.255.0 standby version 2 standby 1 ip 172.16.10.254 exit also see HSRP or FSRP

item.title: VLANs with a trunk (ROAS)
item.description:
en conf t hostname RF-Lab02-SW01 vlan 100 name PRODUCTION-DATA exit vlan 150 name PROD-MANAGEMENT exit vlan 66 name NATIVE-VLAN exit vlan 888 name UNUSED-VLAN exit ip default-gateway 192.168.2.254 interface vlan 100 ip address 192.168.0.100 255.255.254.0 no shut exit interface vlan 150 ip address 192.168.2.100 255.255.255.0 no shut exit interface range f0/1-f0/10 switchport mode access switchport access vlan 100 exit interface range f0/11-f0/20 switchport mode access switchport access vlan 150 exit interface f0/24 switchport mode trunk switchport nonegotiate switchport trunk native vlan 66 switchport trunk allowed vlan 100,150 exit interface range f0/21-f0/23 switchport mode access switchport access vlan 888 shutdown exit en conf t hostname RF-Lab02-SW02 vlan 200 name PRODUCTION-DATA exit vlan 250 name BACKUP-MANAGEMENT exit vlan 66 name NATIVE-VLAN exit vlan 888 name UNUSED-VLAN exit ip default-gateway 172.16.2.254 interface vlan 200 ip address 172.16.0.100 255.255.254.0 no shut exit interface vlan 250 ip address 172.16.2.100 255.255.255.0 no shut exit interface range f0/1-f0/10 switchport mode access switchport access vlan 200 exit interface range f0/11-f0/20 switchport mode access switchport access vlan 250 exit interface f0/24 switchport mode trunk switchport nonegotiate switchport trunk native vlan 66 switchport trunk allowed vlan 200,250 exit interface range f0/21-f0/23 switchport mode access switchport access vlan 888 shutdown exit also see ROAS - Routing on a stick
VLAN access - ROAS

item.title: MISC commands
item.description: Copy run start reload

item.title: Frame forwarding
item.description: Frames layer 2 Runts = frame<64 bytes Giant = frame > 1518 bytes store and forward receives entire frame and dose an error check determines if there are errors first reduces bandwidth of corrupt data cut-through start forwarding before entire frame is received there are 2 types Fast-forward just needs a destination MAC address has more errors Fragment-Free mix between store and forward and cut through error checks the first 64 bytes before forwarding first 64 bytes is where there is most likely to be errors

item.title: Boot sequence
item.description: POST - power on self test - stored in ROM loads the boot loader software Boot loader initalizes the CPU Boot loader initializes the FLASH file system Bootloader loads the OS POST is layer 1 - anything with the physical components Processing (CPU) Memory (RAM) Storage (HDD/SSD) Networking (sometimes optional, NIC) Temp RAM This is volatile - wont survive a shutdown Running config is saved in RAM  Permanent SSD HDD Isn't volatile - will survive a shutdown Know sometimes as ROM - POST is here FLASH is where OS/configs/VLAN database are stored  POST is layer 1 - anything with the physical components LEDs are layer 1 Frames layer 2 Runts = frame<64 bytes Giant = frame > 1518 bytes

item.title: New-ADuser
item.description: New-ADUser [-AccountExpirationDate <DateTime>] [-AccountNotDelegated <Boolean>] [-AccountPassword <SecureString>] [-AllowReversiblePasswordEncryption <Boolean>] [-AuthenticationPolicy <ADAuthenticationPolicy>] [-AuthenticationPolicySilo <ADAuthenticationPolicySilo>] [-AuthType <ADAuthType>] [-CannotChangePassword <Boolean>] [-Certificates <X509Certificate[]>] [-ChangePasswordAtLogon <Boolean>] [-City <String>] [-Company <String>] [-CompoundIdentitySupported <Boolean>] [-Country <String>] [-Credential <PSCredential>] [-Department <String>] [-Description <String>] [-DisplayName <String>] [-Division <String>] [-EmailAddress <String>] [-EmployeeID <String>] [-EmployeeNumber <String>] [-Enabled <Boolean>] [-Fax <String>] [-GivenName <String>] [-HomeDirectory <String>] [-HomeDrive <String>] [-HomePage <String>] [-HomePhone <String>] [-Initials <String>] [-Instance <ADUser>] [-KerberosEncryptionType <ADKerberosEncryptionType>] [-LogonWorkstations <String>] [-Manager <ADUser>] [-MobilePhone <String>] [-Name] <String> [-Office <String>] [-OfficePhone <String>] [-Organization <String>] [-OtherAttributes <Hashtable>] [-OtherName <String>] [-PassThru] [-PasswordNeverExpires <Boolean>] [-PasswordNotRequired <Boolean>] [-Path <String>] [-POBox <String>] [-PostalCode <String>] [-PrincipalsAllowedToDelegateToAccount <ADPrincipal[]>] [-ProfilePath <String>] [-SamAccountName <String>] [-ScriptPath <String>] [-Server <String>] [-ServicePrincipalNames <String[]>] [-SmartcardLogonRequired <Boolean>] [-State <String>] [-StreetAddress <String>] [-Surname <String>] [-Title <String>] [-TrustedForDelegation <Boolean>] [-Type <String>] [-UserPrincipalName <String>] [-WhatIf] [-Confirm] [<CommonParameters>] Import-ADUser

item.title: PowerShell Security and Hashes
item.description: Cyber security Looking after the CIA Confidentiality Only those allowed can read it Integrity Data hasn’t been altered Availability The thing is available when required PowerShell Prevent the user from messing up this is what its based on/the reasoning behind it Set execution policy RemoteSigned If you initiate it then windows assumes you trust the author Undeffined Domain and local Defaults Dose not recognize.ps1 as an exacutable Dose not run a script just by its name Digital signature Certificate Root certificate authority (root/authority CA) Hash Input of any length = fixed length string Cryptographic hash  -- Digital signature Input of any length + secret key = fixed length string Use another key to decode into og string

item.title: Get WMI Computer Info
item.description: #Get-WMIComputerInfo.ps1 #Author: Ryder Fryling #Created: 2026/03/28 #Version 1.0 #Updated: N/A #Description: this script gathers diffrent WMI info from a computer specifyed by the user <# .SYNOPSIS this script gathers diffrent WMI info from a computer specifyed by the user .DESCRIPTION this script gathers diffrent WMI info from a computer specifyed by the user. i can also creat a file in a specifyed location if needed. it will also format some of the data. .PARAMETERInfoClass this is the type of WMI information being collected .PARAMETER Device the device that the information is being collected from .PARAMETER OutFile_Path the swithc for creating a file with the information as well as the path of its location. .EXAMPLE PS C:\Users\bob\Desktop> .\Get-WMIComputerInfo.ps1 -InfoClass LogicalDisk -Device DESKTOP-GQ327M5 DeviceID DriveType ProviderName FreeSpace Size VolumeName -------- --------- ------------ --------- ---- ---------- C: 3 109947891712 135406809088 D: 5 0 6969382912 CPBA_X64FRE_EN-US_DV9 .EXAMPLE PS C:\Users\bob\Desktop> .\Get-WMIComputerInfo.ps1 -OutFile_Path .\ SMBIOSBIOSVersion Manufacturer Name SerialNumber Version ----------------- ------------ ---- ------------ ------- Hyper-V UEFI Release v4.1 Microsoft Corporation Hyper-V UEFI Release v4.1 8546-1777-1739-7800-3950-9116-75 VRTUAL - 1 #> param( [ValidateSet("BIOS", "OS", "Service", "LogicalDisk")] [string] $InfoClass = "BIOS", [string] $Device = "LocalHost", [parameter(Mandatory = $false)] [string] $OutFile_Path ) #sets the variable for the type of data being collected if ($InfoClass -eq "BIOS") { $InfoClass_var = "Win32_Bios" } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "OS"){ $InfoClass_var = "Win32_OperatingSystem" } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "Service"){ $InfoClass_var = "Win32_Service" } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "LogicalDisk"){ $InfoClass_var = "Win32_LogicalDisk" } #creates the formatting for the teble and data if ($InfoClass -eq "BIOS"){ $Table_Data = Select-Object -Property SMBIOSBIOSVersion,Name,Version,SerialNumber,Manufacturer } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "OS"){ $Table_Data = Select-Object -Property SystemDirectory,Organization,BuildNumber,SerialNumber,Version } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "Service"){ $Table_Data = Select-Object -Property Name,ProccessID,StartMode,State } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "LogicalDisk"){ $Table_Data = Select-Object -Property DeviceID,@{name='FreeSpace';expression={[Math]::Round($_.FreeSpace / 1MB, 3)}}, @{name='Size';expression={[math]::Round($_.Size / 1GB, 3)}}, @{name='%Free';expression={[Math]::Round($_.FreeSpace / $_.Size * 100, 3)}} } #collects info $Device_Data = Get-WmiObject -Class $InfoClass_var -ComputerName $Device |Select-Object $Table_Data #creates file name and full path $File_Name = "$($Device)-$($InfoClass)-$(Get-Date -Format yyyy-mm-dd).csv" $OutFile_Path_compleate = $OutFile_Path + "\" + $File_Name #creates file if specifyed if ($OutFile_Path) { Get-WmiObject -Class $InfoClass_var -ComputerName $Device |Select-Object $Table_Data |Export-Csv -Path $OutFile_Path_compleate } #prints data to the terminal $Device_Data |Format-Table -AutoSize -Wrap

item.title: Get CIM Computer Info
item.description: #Get-CIMComputerInfo.ps1 #Author: Ryder Fryling #Created: 2026/03/28 #Version 1.0 #Updated: N/A #Description: this script gatheres information from a inputted list of computers and compiles the collected information in a CSV file. <# .SYNOPSIS this script gatheres information from a inputted list of computers and compiles the collected information in a CSV file. .DESCRIPTION this script gatheres information from a inputted list of computers and compiles the collected information in a CSV file. it will only create the file when a location is given specifying where the file is to be located. .PARAMETER InfoClass this is the type of information that is to be gethered. only one type can be gathered at a time .PARAMETER Device you can input a list of devices to gether information from. this has to be a string. .PARAMETER OutFile_Path This parameter specifyes the location of where the CSV file is to be created. it is also the switch for the creation of a CSV file. .EXAMPLE PS C:\Users\bob\Desktop> .\Get-CIMComputerInfo.ps1 -Device DESKTOP-GQ327M5,LocalHost -InfoClass LogicalDisk -OutFile_Path .\ Win32_LogicalDisk: C: (DeviceID = "C:") Win32_LogicalDisk: D: (DeviceID = "D:") DeviceID DriveType ProviderName VolumeName Size FreeSpace PSComputerName -------- --------- ------------ ---------- ---- --------- -------------- C: 3 135406809088 109951479808 DESKTOP-GQ327M5 D: 5 CPBA_X64FRE_EN-US_DV9 6969382912 0 DESKTOP-GQ327M5 C: 3 135406809088 109951479808 LocalHost D: 5 CPBA_X64FRE_EN-US_DV9 6969382912 0 LocalHost LastWriteTime : 3/29/2026 11:51:01 PM Length : 0 Name : WS-Info-CIM-LogicalDisk-2026-51-29.csv .EXAMPLE PS C:\Users\bob\Desktop> .\Get-CIMComputerInfo.ps1 -Device LocalHost -InfoClass BIOS -Start_RM SMBIOSBIOSVersion Manufacturer Name SerialNumber Version PSComputerName ----------------- ------------ ---- ------------ ------- -------------- Hyper-V UEFI Release v4.1 Microsoft Corporation Hyper-V UEFI Release v4.1 8546-1777-1739-7800-3950-9116-75 VRTUAL - 1 LocalHost #> param( [ValidateSet("BIOS", "OS", "Service", "LogicalDisk")] [string] $InfoClass = "LogicalDisk", [array] $Device = "LocalHost", [parameter(Mandatory = $false)] [string] $OutFile_Path, [parameter(Mandatory = $false)] [switch] $Start_RM ) #matches the class to the inputed parameter if ($InfoClass -eq "BIOS") { $InfoClass_var = "CIM_BIOSElement" } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "OS"){ $InfoClass_var = "CIM_OperatingSystem" } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "Service"){ $InfoClass_var = "CIM_Service" } elseif ($InfoClass -eq "LogicalDisk"){ $InfoClass_var = "CIM_LogicalDisk"} #starts WinRM on the local host if needed if ($Start_RM){ Start-Service -Name WinRM } #creates terminal output $Device | ForEach-Object { Get-CimInstance -ComputerName $_ -ClassName $InfoClass_var |Format-Table -AutoSize -Wrap } #creates the outfile if seleced if ($OutFile_Path) { #creates the file name and full path $File_Name = "WS-Info-CIM-$($InfoClass)-$(Get-Date -Format yyyy-mm-dd).csv" $OutFile_Path_compleate = $OutFile_Path + "\" + $File_Name #crteates the file New-Item -Path $OutFile_Path -Name $File_Name -ItemType File #loops through th inputted devices and adds it to the file $Device | ForEach-Object{ Get-CimInstance -ComputerName $_ -ClassName $InfoClass_var |Export-Csv -Path $OutFile_Path_compleate -NoTypeInformation -Append } }

item.title: Get User Login History
Get Service Health Report
item.description: #Get-ServiceHealthReport.ps1 #Author: Ryder Fryling #Created: 2026/03/22 #Version 1.0 #Updated: N/A #Description: This script Audits Windows services by status, startup type, and service account. <# .SYNOPSIS Audits Windows services by status, startup type, and service account. .DESCRIPTION Queries Windows services using Get-Service and Get-CimInstance, then filters and reports based on the specified criteria. Useful for identifying stopped auto-start services, services running under non-standard accounts, or summarizing overall service health. .PARAMETER Status Filter services by status. Valid values: Running, Stopped, All. Defaults to All. .PARAMETER StartupType Filter by startup type. Valid values: Automatic, Manual, Disabled, All. Defaults to All. .PARAMETER AccountFilter If specified, shows only services running under this account name (partial match). For example, "LocalSystem" or "Network". .PARAMETER SortBy Property to sort results by. Valid values: Name, Status, StartType. Defaults to Name. .EXAMPLE .\Get-ServiceHealthReport.ps1 Lists all services sorted by name. .EXAMPLE .\Get-ServiceHealthReport.ps1 -Status Stopped -StartupType Automatic Shows only stopped services that are configured to start automatically (potential problems that need attention). .EXAMPLE .\Get-ServiceHealthReport.ps1 -AccountFilter "LocalSystem" -SortBy Status Shows services running as LocalSystem, sorted by their current status. #> param( [validateSet("Running", "Stopped", "All")] [string]$Status = "All", [validateSet("Automatic", "Manual", "Disabled", "Auto", "All")] [string]$StartupType = "All", [Parameter(Mandatory = $False)] [string]$AccountFilter, [validateSet("Name", "Status", "StartType", "StartMode", "State")] [string]$SortBy = "Name" ) #gets the services $services = Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Service #sorts them by status if ($Status -ne "All") { $services = $services | Where-Object { $_.State -eq $Status } } #sorts them by start type if ($StartupType -ne "All") { $services = $services | Where-Object { $_.StartMode -eq $StartupType } } #filters the accounts to best match user input if ($AccountFilter) { $services = $services | Where-Object { $_.StartName -like "*$AccountFilter*" } } #formats the $SortBy to be valid input options if ($SortBy -eq "Status") { $SortBy = "State" } elseif ($SortBy -eq "StartType") { $SortBy = "StartMode" } #sorts the data then makes the table and formats it $services | Sort-Object -Property $SortBy | ForEach-Object { [PSCustomObject]@{ Name = $_.Name DisplayName = $_.DisplayName Status = $_.State StartType = $_.StartMode Account = $_.StartName } } | Format-Table -Property Name,Status,StartType,Account,DisplayName -AutoSize -Wrap

item.title: Get Disk Report
item.description: #Get-DiskReport.ps1 #Author: Ryder Fryling #Created: 2026/03/19 #Version 1.0 #Updated: N/A #Description: This script reports disk space for one or more drives, with output format controlled by a parameter. <# .SYNOPSIS This script reports disk space for one or more drives .DESCRIPTION This script reports disk space for one or more drives, with output format controlled by a parameter to set the measurment in GB or MB. #> param( [string[]]$driveletter, [Validateset("GB", "MB")] [string]$unit = "GB" ) #formates the user input to match powershell input $DataUnit = if ($unit -eq "GB") { 1GB } else { 1MB } #collects the data Get-Volume -DriveLetter $driveletter | ForEach-Object{ [PSCustomObject]@{ #formates the table with some math Drive = $_.DriveLetter "Total($unit)" = [math]::Round($_.Size / $DataUnit, 2) "Free($unit)" = [math]::Round($_.SizeRemaining / $DataUnit, 2 ) PercentFree = [math]::Round(($_.SizeRemaining / $_.Size) * 100, 2) } }|Format-Table -AutoSize

item.title: ADDS Setup
item.description:
Preconfig Name Ip Add role Select AD DS Keep additional  management features  Add DNS at the same time Install Post deployment Flag next to manage Promote server Name the domain in a forrest Select add new forrest Set the root domain Select backwards functionality Check DNS and GC Set the DSRM password Don’t create a DNS delegation Verify the NetBios domain name Next next Pass prereq Install Setup DNS Most of the DNS setup is done automatically Create primary RLZ Create a replication scope Follow DNS and allow auto updates Update records Add DHCP Follow normal setup Add the parent domain On PC Join the environment Join the domain System info Join domain or work group Use creds for domain admin Restart Create user on AD DS Tools > AD users and comps Click on domain > new organizational unit Give it a name Uncheck protect from accidental deletion - for lab Repeat under the unit you just created for groups Under last one create new user Give name and password Click on properties add to groups Repeat for other users appropriately also see
Install ADDS

item.title: DHCP setup
item.description:
Server mgr First give name No active directory Give it a IP and IPv6 Serv role Add role Roll based Select roll + management features Don’t restart Install DHCP tab Click more on the warning Press complete config Press commit Press close Tools > DHCP Create scope IPv4 Right click on ipv4 > new scope Give it a name and description Enter ip add scope +SNM Add exclusions if applicable Set the lese duration Additional options Enter DG Add DNS WINS server -skip cuz legacy Click next to finish verify also see DHCP Failover
What is DHCP
Install DHCP

item.title: Reset AD User Password
item.description: #Reset-ADUserPassword.ps1 #Your Name Here #modifyed by Ryder Fryling #Created March 6, 2019 #Last Modified mar 14, 2026 #Synopsis #This script resets a given AD user's password and forces a password change at next logon. #Script could be modified to allow for a string of users to reset multiple users at once. #Set Variables Get-ADUser -Filter * | Format-Table -Property name $user = "" $ulist = @() #creates the array of users whos passwords are to be reset while ($user -ne "next") { $user = Read-Host "Enter logon name of the user for whom you'd like to reset the password (type `'next`' to continue)" #adds user to the array unless the input is equal to exit if ($user -ne "next") { $ulist += "$user" } } #Execution #loop to reset the passwords $ulist | foreach { Set-ADUser -Identity $_ -ChangePasswordAtLogon $true -WhatIf } <# .SYNOPSIS This script resets a given AD user's password and forces a password change at next logon. .DESCRIPTION the script allow the user to input multiple users to reset the password for. they can continue by typing exit. the script will then create a array and cycle through it to reset the passwords of the inputted users. #>

item.title: Get AD Account Info
item.description: #Get-ADAccounts.ps1 #Your Name Here #modifyed by Ryder Fryling #Created March 6, 2018 #Last Modified Date mar 14, 2026 #Synopsis #This script retrieves a list of all the active or disabled user accounts in the active directory domain. #Variables/Parameters $AccountType = Read-Host "Do you want a list of active or disabled accounts? (`"A`" for Active, `"D`" for Disabled)" #Execution # chooses weather the user selected acive accounts of dissabled accounts if ($AccountType -eq "A") { $TF = $true }elseif ($AccountType -eq "D") { $TF = $false } #Retrieves the desired user accounts, depending on user choice. Get-ADUser -Filter {Enabled -eq $TF} | Format-Table -Property Name,Enabled <# .SYNOPSIS This script retrieves a list of all the active or disabled user accounts in the active directory domain. .DESCRIPTION you can choose to get a list of either all Active or dissabled accounts, then the script outputs a list of all accounts in that catagory. #>

item.title: Cleanup Old Files
item.description: #Cleanup-OldFiles.ps1 #Author Skulky Tim #modifyed by Ryder Fryling #Created February 27, 2019 #Last Modified mar 14, 2026 #Rationale and Synopsis #The intention is to locate all the files not used within a specified date in a current directory. #Once the files are found, user should be given the chance to delete them. #Current status: Kind of sort of halfish done, but execution not working as expected #It would also be nice if user could select how old the files should be (e.g. older than 30 days, 10 days, 60 days, etc.) #Variables $days = Read-Host "enter how many days ago you would like to search" $filepath = (Get-Location) $olderthandate = (Get-Date).AddDays(-$days) #Execution #creates the table Get-ChildItem -Path $filepath -Recurse | Where-Object {$_.LastWriteTime -le $olderthandate} | Format-Table -Property Name,LastWriteTime #decision to delete the files if ((Read-Host "delete the Files?") -contains "yes" -or "y") { #gets the files and deletes them Get-ChildItem -Path $filepath -Recurse | Where-Object {$_.LastWriteTime -le $olderthandate} | remove-item -WhatIf }else{ #seccond option saying the files wernt deleted Write-Host "the files wre not deleted" } <# .SYNOPSIS The intention is to locate all the files not used within a specified date in a current directory. .DESCRIPTION The intention is to locate all the files not used within a specified date in a current directory. Once the files are found, user should be given the chance to delete them. Current status: Kind of sort of halfish done, but execution not working as expected It would also be nice if user could select how old the files should be (e.g. older than 30 days, 10 days, 60 days, etc.) #>

item.title: Fix Print Spooler
item.description: #Fix-PrintSpooler.ps1 #Author Skulky Tim #modifyed by Ryder Fryling #Created February 27, 2019 #Last Modified mar 14, 2026 #Rationale and Synopsis #The intention of the script is to ensure the print spooler service is running at all times. #This script will check to see if the spooler is running. #If the print spooler is not running, it needs to be started. #If the print spooler is already running, it should be restarted for good measure. #Variables #Execution #while loop to keep the script running while ($true) { #checks if the spooler is running and starts it if it is not if ((Get-Service -Name Spooler| Select-Object -Property status) -eq "Running" ) { Start-Service -Name Spooler } else { #restarts thje spooler if its running Restart-Service -Name Spooler -Force } #wait timer and prints the status Wait-Event -Timeout 1 Get-Service -Name Spooler } <# .SYNOPSIS The intention of the script is to ensure the print spooler service is running at all times. .DESCRIPTION This script will check to see if the spooler is running. If the print spooler is not running, it needs to be started. If the print spooler is already running, it should be restarted for good measure. #>

item.title: Kill Proccess
item.description: #Kill-Process.ps1 #Author Skulky Tim #modifyed by Ryder Fryling #Created February 27, 2019 #Last Modified mar 14, 2026 #Rationale and Synopsis #The intention of the script is to allow the user to select a running process from a list and pretend kill it. #Current status - loop works, needs followup #Variables #collects input for which pocess we want $letter = Read-Host "Let's pretend to kill a process.`n`nPlease enter a letter of the alphabet" #gets the processes and prepairs them for output $array = (Get-Process -Name $letter*) $counter = 1 #Execution #output for the head of the table Write-Host "These are the current running processes starting with the letter $letter`:" # creates a table of the collected processes ForEach ($process in $array) { Write-Output "[$counter] $($process.name)" $counter++ } #Lets the user choose a process, then stop it with a -WhatIf $chosen = Read-Host "choose a process tro pretend kill (enter the number)" Stop-Process -Name ($array[($chosen -1)]).Name -WhatIf <# .SYNOPSIS The intention of the script is to allow the user to select a running process from a list and pretend kill it. .DESCRIPTION the script allows the user to select a bunch of processes statrting with a letter. it creates a list from a inputted letter then we can select a process from that list and see what could happen if we killed it #>

item.title: ADDS User Audit
Install DHCP
item.description: # Script Name: Install-DHCP.ps1 # Author: Ryder Fryling # Date Created: 2/26/2026 # Synopsis: this script installs the DHCP server roll then creates a scope from the requested data. #input from the user for the scope $servip = Read-Host "enter the ip of this server" $rangeS = Read-Host "enter the start of the DHCP range" $rangeE = Read-Host "enter the end of the DHCP range" $DG = Read-Host "enter the Deafault gateway" $scopeN = Read-Host "enter the name of the scope" $SNM = Read-Host "enter the subnet mask" #installs the server roll Install-WindowsFeature -Name DHCP -IncludeManagementTools -IncludeAllSubFeature #imports the modual and adds the server roll to this domain controller Import-Module DhcpServer Add-DhcpServerInDC #creates the scope with the requirements from the user. Add-DhcpServerv4Scope -Name $scopeN -StartRange $rangeS -EndRange $rangeE -SubnetMask $SNM Set-DhcpServerv4OptionValue -ScopeId $rangeS -DnsServer $servip -Router $DG -ScopeId $rangeS -DnsServer $servip -Router $DG

item.title: Install ADDS
item.description: # Script Name: install-ADDS.ps1 # Author: Ryder Fryling # Date Created: 2/26/2026 # Synopsis: this script installs the AD DS server role and configures the domain. it also adds the DNS role. #collects info from the user regarding the namespace $netbios = Read-Host "enter the NetBIOS name" $SNspace = Read-Host "enter the specific namespace" $TLD = Read-Host "enter the top level domain" $DN = $SNspace + "." + $netbios + "." + $TLD #installs the active directory role Install-WindowsFeature -Name AD-Domain-Services -IncludeManagementTools #imports the active directory modual Import-Module ADDSDeployment #installs the domain controller and DNS with the domain name Install-ADDSDomainController -InstallDns -DomainName $DN Install-ADDSForest -DomainName $DN -DomainNetbiosName $netbios

item.title: Profile script
item.description: New-PSDrive -PSProvider FileSystem -Root "C:\Users\bob\Desktop\PowerShell Practice" -Name "PSPractice" New-Alias -Name "NetAd" Get-NetAdapter New-Alias -Name "ELog" Get-EventLog New-Alias -Name "pros" Get-Process Import-Alias -Path C:\Users\bob\Desktop\ShowMe.txt cd PSPractice: clear Write-Host "Welcome to PowerShell v5.1"

item.title: layer 3 Switch OSPF v2 example
item.description: en conf t hostname RF-137-L06-SW01 ip routing vlan 400 name Data exit vlan 999 name BLACK-HOLE exit int f0/23 no switchport ip address 10.1.1.9 255.255.255.252 no shut exit int f0/24 no switchport ip address 10.1.1.5 255.255.255.252 no shut exit int vlan 400 ip address 192.168.4.254 255.255.255.0 no shut exit int f0/1 switchport mode access switchport access vlan 400 no shut exit int lo2 ip address 192.168.3.254 255.255.255.0 no shut exit router ospf 2 network 10.1.1.8 0.0.0.3 area 0 network 10.1.1.4 0.0.0.3 area 0 network 192.168.4.254 0.0.0.0 area 0 network 192.168.3.254 0.0.0.0 area 0 passive-interface f0/1 passive-interface lo2 router 3.3.3.3 exit exit clear ip ospf process this connects with OSPFv2-example
item.guid: ICT 137/Routing/Layer 3 Switches/Configs/layer 3 Switch OSPF v2 example.md

item.title: OSPFv2-example
item.description: en conf t hostname RF-137-L06-RT01 int g0/1 ip address 10.1.1.10 255.255.255.252 no shut exit int g0/0 ip address 10.1.1.1 25.255.255.252 no shut exit int lo 2 ip address 192.168.1.254 255.255.255.0 no shut exit router ospf 2 network 10.1.1.8 0.0.0.3 area 0 network 10.1.1.0 0.0.0.3 area 0 network 192.168.1.254 0.0.0.0 area 0 passive-interface lo2 router 1.1.1.1 exit exit clear ip ospf process en conf t hostname RF-137-L06-RT02 int g0/1 ip address 10.1.1.6 255.255.255.252 no shut exit int g0/0 ip address 10.1.1.2 25.255.255.252 no shut exit int lo 2 ip address 192.168.2.254 255.255.255.0 no shut exit router ospf 2 network 10.1.1.4 0.0.0.3 area 0 network 10.1.1.0 0.0.0.3 area 0 network 192.168.2.254 0.0.0.0 area 0 passive-interface lo2 router 2.2.2.2 exit exit clear ip ospf process this connects to layer 3 Switch OSPF v2 example

item.title: VLAN access - ROAS
item.description: en conf t hostname RF-Lab02-RT01 interface g0/0/0 no shut exit interface g0/0/0.100 encapsulation dot1q 100 ip address 192.168.1.254 255.255.254.0 exit interface g0/0/0.150 encapsulation dot1q 150 ip address 192.168.2.254 255.255.255.0 exit interface g0/0/1 ip address 10.0.0.1 255.255.255.252 no shut exit ip route 172.16.0.0 255.255.254.0 10.0.0.2 ip route 172.16.2.0 255.255.255.0 10.0.0.2 en conf t hostname RF-Lab02-RT02 interface g0/0/0 no shut exit interface g0/0/0.200 encapsulation dot1q 200 ip address 172.16.1.254 255.255.254.0 exit interface g0/0/0.250 encapsulation dot1q 250 ip address 172.16.2.254 255.255.255.0 exit interface g0/0/1 ip address 10.0.0.2 255.255.255.252 no shut exit ip route 192.168.0.0 255.255.254.0 10.0.0.1 ip route 192.168.2.0 255.255.255.0 10.0.0.1

item.title: RIP2 - config
item.description: en conf t hostname Kel-RT interface serial 0/0/1 ip address 10.20.30.34 255.255.255.252 no shutdown exit interface serial 0/1/0 ip address 10.20.30.38 255.255.255.252 no shutdown exit interface serial 0/1/1 ip address 10.20.30.45 255.255.255.252 no shutdown exit interface serial 0/0/0 ip address 10.20.30.42 255.255.255.252 no shutdown exit router rip version 2 no auto-summary network 10.20.30.32 network 10.20.30.36 network 10.20.30.40 network 10.20.30.44 exit do show ip protocol

item.title: OU info
item.description:
Commands
Best Practices-OUs
Scripting-OU's

item.title: Remove-ADUser
Remove-ADOU's
item.description: # Script Name: Remove-ADOrganizationUnit.ps1 # Author: Danit Kang # Date Created: 03/22/2026 # Date Modified: 03/22/2026 # Synopsis: Removes all AD OU's Recursively $ADSearch = Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter * -SearchBase "OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" -SearchScope Subtree # Disables Protect from Accidental Deletion for all OU's $ADSearch | Set-ADOrganizationalUnit -ProtectedFromAccidentalDeletion $false # Removes OU's $ADSearch | Remove-ADOrganizationalUnit -Recursive

item.title: Create-OU's
item.description: # Script Name: Create-AtlasOU.ps1 # Author: Danit Kang # Date Created: 03/23/2026 # Date Modified: 03/24/2026 # Synopsis: Creates new Atlas OU Structure # First Hierarchical Level of OUs New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Atlas" -Path "DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Devices" -Path "OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "IT" -Path "OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Staff" -Path "OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" # Inside Devices OU New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Servers" -Path "OU=Devices,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Workstations" -Path "OU=Devices,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" # Inside IT OU New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "IT-Admin" -Path "OU=IT,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "IT-Staff" -Path "OU=IT,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" # Inside Staff OU New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Executive Management" -Path "OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Regular Staff" -Path "OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" # Inside Management-Admin OU New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Operations Managers" -Path "OU=Executive Management,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Owners" -Path "OU=Executive Management,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" # Inside Regular Staff OU New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "C-Suite" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Dispatch" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Finance" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "HR" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Maintenance" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Marketing-PR" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Administration" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Receptionists" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Safety-Compliance" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Sales-PR" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Transport" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "VPs" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com" New-ADOrganizationalUnit -Name "Warehouse" -Path "OU=Regular Staff,OU=Staff,OU=Atlas,DC=Corp,DC=Atlas,DC=com"

item.title: Import-ADUser
item.description: # date created: 05/03/2026 # date modified: 16/03/2026 # author: Erik Bjerstedt # # Synopsis: automatically creates AD users on the system from a CSV file # # Warning: This script assumes that your OU structure and naming (including capitalization) is identical to the project's ADDS diagram. # Before running, verify that your OUs are structured correctly. # # Citations: # ICT 128 Lab 07 - Full lab.pdf - Phase 3 used as reference # defining default password $defaultPass = ConvertTo-SecureString "S@lta5002!" -AsPlainText -Force # importing the csv $userList = Import-Csv -Path (Read-Host "Enter your CSV file's full path") Write-Host "$($userList.Count) users loaded from CSV." # processing CSV users foreach ($user in $userList) { # getting name $fName = $user.'First Name'.Trim() $lName = $user.'Last Name'.Trim() $fullName = "$fName.$lName" # building SAM and UPN $sam = ($fullName -replace '[^a-zA-Z0-9]', '').ToLower() $upn = "$sam@atlas.com" # building path $subDept = $user.Subdepartment $title = $user.Title if ($subDept.Length -gt 5) {$subDept = $subDept.Substring(0, 5)} forEach ($OU in (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -like "*"})) { if ($title.Contains("Owner")) { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -eq "Owners"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($title.Contains("VP")) { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -eq "VPs"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($title.Contains("Operations")) { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -like "Operations*"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($title -ceq "CIO") { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -eq "C-Suite"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($subDept -eq "Accou") { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -eq "Finance"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($SubDept -eq ("Administration")) { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -eq "Administration"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($title.Contains("Administrator")) { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -like "IT-Admin*"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($title.Contains("Technician")) { $OUPath = (Get-ADOrganizationalUnit -Filter {Name -eq "IT-Staff"}).DistinguishedName break } elseif ($OU.Name.Contains($subDept)) { $OUPath = $OU.DistinguishedName break } } # creating ADuser New-ADUser ` -GivenName $fName ` -Surname $lName ` -Name $fullName ` -DisplayName "$fName $lName" ` -SamAccountName $sam ` -UserPrincipalName $upn ` -Department $user.Department ` -Title $user.Title ` -AccountPassword $defaultPass ` -EmployeeID $user.'Employee ID' ` -Office $user.'Office Location' ` -OfficePhone $user.'Office Local Phone' ` -EmailAddress $upn ` -ChangePasswordAtLogon $true ` -Enabled $true ` -Path "$OUPath" # confirming creation $created = Get-ADUser -Identity $sam -Properties Enabled -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue if ($created){ # if user creation succeeds Write-Host "$sam successfully created (Enabled: $($created.Enabled))" -ForegroundColor Green Write-Host " SubDepartment: $($subDept), OU: $($OUPath)" } else { # if user creation fails Write-Host "Failed: $sam" -ForegroundColor Red } #reseting path $OUPath = "" } # finish message Write-Host "`nImport complete. $($userList.Count) users processed."

item.title: Role information

ADDS AAA infrastructure
Domain and forest structure
What is DHCP
what is RASS
what is DFS

item.title: DHCP Failover
item.description:
Follow setup step 1-4 Serv 1 Right click scope > config failover Next Select alternate server Way 1 Enter ip add Way 2 Click add server Brows for it Name the relationship Select mode Load balance They both respond to requests Select split Hot standby Second serv only responds in the event that the first one dose not work Enable authentication > enter password Click finish Verify Check on serve 2 (may need refresh) Verify network Use following commands on pc as admin Ipconfig /release Ipconfig /renew Ipconfig /all also see What is DHCP
DHCP setup

item.title: What is DHCP
item.description:
What is DHCP Dynamic host control protocol Dynamically distributes all TCP info Process Sent broadcast discover request using DORA  If failed uses a APIPA address (automatic previsioned IP address) Packet is dropped by non DHCP servers DHCP sends back a packet with an offer address If multiple usually takes the first off Sends back a request packet DHCP sends ack message With Microsoft devices the follow up can be a unicast Scope Pools of address Router conf to forward DHCP requestsEn Conf t Int [out port] Ip helper address [ DHCP ip] Conf DHCP in server Is a role We can do scope + more The pool also see
DHCP setup
DHCP Failover
item.guid: ICT 127/Server info/Server roles/Role info/What is DHCP.md

item.title: ADDS AAA infrastructure
item.description:
Active directory -- AAA infrastructure = domain based environment  Authentication Centralizes our users/credentials Authorization Principle of least privilege Control rights and privileges of all users Group policy Accounting Auditing is easy AD DS highhearted Domain Tree Small domains that share the same root domain Forrest A group of domain trees also see
ADDS Setup
ADDS Redundancy config
OU info
Domain and forest structure
item.guid: ICT 127/Server info/Server roles/Role info/ADDS AAA infrastructure.md

item.title: VPN info
item.description: VPN Consumer version of a VPN OSINT - open source intelligence S2S vs C2S C2S -client to site Client and connect to a specific site (LAN) Create an encrypted connection Temporary connection  S2S -site to site Connection between 2 sites that allows for all intersite traffic Ongoing connection (always on) also see what is RASS

item.title: Built in variables
item.description: $env:USERNAME name of the current user $env:USERPROFILE current user profile. contains the current directory $profile the profile of the PowerShell session it runs when you start PowerShell you can use it to customize PSDrives and your start location

item.title: creating parameters in scripts
item.description: parameters run at the start of the script. so put them at the start right under help. param( [ValidateRange(1-100)] #valid input for the parameter [int]$example1 = 1, #[int] is the data type, $example1 is a variable that is the name of the parameter, = 1 is the [Parameter(Mandatory = $False)] [string] $example2 )

item.title: Pasted image 20260323135330
Start base for a script
item.description: #[script name].ps1 #Author: Ryder Fryling #Created: yyyy/mm/dd #Version 1.0 #Updated: N/A #Description: This script... <# .SYNOPSIS short description .DESCRIPTION long/detailed description .PARAMETER [parameter] Info about parameter .EXAMPLE example of powershell terminal input and output #>

item.title: What is Multiplexing
item.description: it is a method of increasing bandwidth among clients through different types mediums. this is done through multiple different methods. combining multiple input signals into one signal.

item.title: Multiplexing
item.description:

WDM - Wavelength Division Multiplexing
FDM - Frequency Division Multiplexing
TDM - Time Division Multiplexing
CDM - Code Division Multiplexing

item.title: Lab Code (Karls)
item.description:
L03
L04
L05
L06
L07
Collected Lab Code (Karls)

item.title: Collected Lab Code (Karls)
L06
L05
L04
item.description: //define variables int count = 0; // the setup function runs once when you press reset or power the board void setup() { //initializa serial communication (serial port on USB) Serial.begin(9600); // initialize digital pin LED_BUILTIN as an output. pinMode(LED_BUILTIN, OUTPUT); } // the loop function runs over and over again forever void loop() { digitalWrite(LED_BUILTIN, HIGH); // turn the LED on (HIGH is the voltage level) count++; //increase the count by 1 Serial.println(count); //send the count through serial port delay(1000); // wait for a second digitalWrite(LED_BUILTIN, LOW); // turn the LED off by making the Voltage LOW delay(1000); // wait for a second } import serial ser = serial.Serial('/dev/ttyACM0',9600) while True: readedText = ser.readline().decode('utf-8').rstrip() print(readedText) import serial #set the serial port to ttyS0 ser=serial.Serial('/dev/ttyS0',115200) while True: readedText = ser.readline().decode('utf-8').rstrip() try: #send STOP to reset counter if the count divided by 10 if int(readedText) % 10 == 0: ser.write(b"STOP\n") print(readedText) except: print(readedText) //define variable int count = 0; // the setup function runs once when you press reset or power the board void setup() { //initialize serial communication on pin 13, 14 Serial1.begin(115200); // initialize digital pin LED_BUILTIN as an output. pinMode(LED_BUILTIN, OUTPUT); } // the loop function runs over and over again forever void loop() { //Receive the incoming message if (Serial1.available() > 0){ String data = Serial1.readStringUntil('\n'); //if the messsage is STOP, reset the counter if (data == "STOP"){ count = 0; //send the message "Counter Reset!" Serial1.println("Counter Reset!"); } } digitalWrite(LED_BUILTIN, HIGH); // turn the LED on (HIGH is the voltage level) count++; Serial1.println(count); //send the count delay(1000); // wait for a second digitalWrite(LED_BUILTIN, LOW); // turn the LED off by making the voltage LOW delay(1000); // wait for a second }

item.title: L07
item.description: from gpiozero import PWMLED, MCP3008 from time import sleep pot = MCP3008(0) led = PWMLED(14) while True: if(pot.value < 0.001): led.value = 0 else: led.value = pot.value print(pot.value) sleep(0.1) # potential change """pot = MCP3008(channel=7, select_pin=19, mosi_pin=5, miso_pin=6, clock_pin=13) """

item.title: L03
item.description: import RPi.GPIO as GPIO import time GPIO.setwarnings(False) GPIO.setmode(GPIO.BCM) GPIO.setup(20,GPIO.OUT) # Set pin 21 to be an input pin and set initial value to be pulled low (off) GPIO.setup(21, GPIO.IN, pull_up_down=GPIO.PUD_DOWN) count = 0 GPIO.output(20,GPIO.LOW) while True: # Run forever if GPIO.input(21) == GPIO.LOW: count+=1 print(count, "Button was pushed!") GPIO.output(20,GPIO.HIGH) time.sleep(1) GPIO.output(20,GPIO.LOW)

item.title: Data transfer Equations
item.description: T = Time of symbol B = BaudFs = Sampling Frequency T = period of the sample N = Number of levels in the ADC n = Bit size of the ADC

item.title: Code
item.description:
My own Code
Lab Code (Karls)

item.title: Basic formula
item.description: I = current (Amps) Q = charge (coulombs) T = time (s)V = volts I = current R = resistance P = Power (wats) E = Energy (Jouls) T = Time (s) R = Resistance (Ohms) I = current (Amps) V = voltage

ICT 123
item.description:
Code
Data Protocols
Equations, Math and Laws

item.title: Laws
item.description: Kirchhoff's Law

item.title: Kirchhoff's Law
item.description: Kirchhoff's current law Total current going into a junction = the total amount going out I = I1 + I2 +… Kirchhoff's voltage law Kirchhoff's voltage law states that, given any two specific points on a circuit at a particular time, the difference in voltage between the two points (known as the voltage drop) is the same no matter what pathway you take to get there.

item.title: Unit Conversions
Pasted image 20260318155635
Get-CimInstance
item.description: this is used like a Get-_______ where [____] is equal to some sort of process/service/computer object is dictated. that part in Get-CimInstance is dictated by the class/namespace/instance. Get-CimInstance -Namespace root -ClassName __Namespace | select-object -ExpandProperty Name | sort-object

item.title: CIM vs WMI vs Native PS CMDlets
item.description: It is a Windows service that exposes hardware, operating system components, and software as structured objects that PowerShell (and other tools) can query. WMI has been built into Windows since Windows 2000. WMI is the windows version of CMI. it is no longer being developed or supported it is being phased out and is not in PS 7 It is a vendor-neutral standard published by the Distributed Management Task Force (DMTF) that defines how system components should be described as objects. WMI is Microsoft's Windows-specific implementation of this standard. the universal version of WMI used to get info about the OS is not available on windows 7 or older you will need to use WMI on those OS's cannot be piped ( | ) Namespaces are the top-level containers. They group related classes together. The most important namespace for network and system administration is root\CIMV2. PowerShell uses this namespace by default when you run Get-CimInstance without specifying one.Other namespaces serve specialised purposes: root\SecurityCenter2: antivirus and firewall product information root\WMI: hardware performance data root\MicrosoftDNS: DNS server management (on DNS servers only) My summery This is like the Directory/folder that the data is located in. Classes define the structure of a type of system component. A class lists all the properties that objects of that type have, along with their data types. Class names follow a convention: Win32_*: provided by the Win32 provider; covers OS, hardware, and networking CIM_*: base classes from the CIM standard; Win32 classes inherit from these __* (double underscore): WMI system classes, metadata about WMI itself My Summery this is the type/format of where the data comes from Instances are the actual data objects, one instance per real component matching the class template. The class Win32_NetworkAdapter defines what properties a network adapter object has; each installed adapter (physical and virtual) is a separate instance of that class.Note Some classes always return exactly one instance (Win32_OperatingSystem) there is only one operating system. Others return many (Win32_NetworkAdapter) one per adapter. Knowing which to expect is part of reading query output correctly.
item.guid: ICT 128/Windows Info/CIM vs WMI vs Native PS CMDlets.md

item.title: PowerShell commands
item.description:
New-ADUser (ActiveDirectory) | Microsoft Learn
Select-Object
New-ADuser
CIM vs Native cmdlets
CIM cmdlets
WMI cmdlet (legacy)
Get-CimInstance

item.title: Get the verions
item.description: $PSVersionTable.PSVersion

item.title: WQL
item.description: WQL (WMI Query Language) is a read-only query language for WMI, modelled on SQL. It uses SELECT, FROM, and WHERE to specify what data to retrieve.In PowerShell, WQL is used with the -Filter parameter (which accepts the WHERE clause) or the -Query parameter (which accepts a full WQL statement):# Using -Filter (WHERE clause only, class specified separately) Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_Service -Filter "State='Running'" # Using -Query (full WQL statement) Get-CimInstance -Query "SELECT * FROM Win32_Service WHERE State='Running'"

item.title: WMI cmdlet (legacy)
item.description: Get-WmiObject -Class Win32_OperatingSystem

item.title: CIM cmdlets
item.description: Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_OperatingSystem

item.title: IT value
item.description: IT value and worth Worth or desirability of a tech process, service or product Subjective assessment Depends on how the business/manager views it Tied to the business model Adding value should enable the company to do its business better We need to make a meaningful, tangible difference (ROI, KPI)

item.title: creating help in comments
item.description: use <# at the start for the block comment and #> at the end to close it. .SOMETHING is how you specify what that bit of text if for in the help

item.title: Installing RRAS
item.description: Add role and feature Select RRAS Rest as normal Run the getting started wizard --optional --VPN wizard --not the best Click on config routing and remote access --tools Click on cloud > wizard Custom Select LAN routing Next Next Turn service on Same --VPN Custom Select VPN and demand dial Next Next Ignore error Click finish Config encapsulation protocol -LT2P Right click on server > properties Click yes for method Create a shared key Apply Create address pool Select IPv4 Enter the range that doesn't interfere with the DHCP scope Create demand dial int Right click > new demand dial int The name is the name of the device you are connecting to Select encapsulation method --same as before Enter the public/wan IP Select local authentication Config the route Config trustworthiness Create creds

item.title: Pasted image 20260316114524
Pasted image 20260316113650
OSI model
item.description: OSI POST is layer 1 - anything with the physical components LEDs are layer 1 Frames layer 2 Runts = frame<64 bytes Giant = frame > 1518 bytes

item.title: LED indicators
item.description: LED codes We are most concerned with green, blinking green, amber, and alternating green/amber STAT  amber/blinking amber - something is being blocked STAT alternating green and amber - something wrong with the port PoE - green means its working

item.title: Best Practices-OUs
item.description: Keep is a simple as possible Alphabetization Consider starting with the default OU structure that troy uses Always remember the strategic value of security groups Users --> groups --> OU's <---- GPOs Naming convention Simple is better Pay attention to the typical behavior By default GPO's are inherited to lower Ous Base OU build --Troys setup Default domain policy (no touchy) WS Win 10 Win 11 Servers  Non DC servers Personnel IT Admins Techs Staff General user settings Domain controllers Default domain controller policy (no touchy)

item.title: DNS sharing or Trusts
item.description: FLZ > properties -on eggs Zone transfers Only to the following servers Edit > enter IP's FLZ -bacon New zone Secondary Enter zone name on eggs Enter IP of eggs Repeat for RLZ
item.guid: ICT 127/Server info/Server roles/Role configurations/DNS sharing or Trusts.md

item.title: DNS setup
item.description: Core config (name, ip, etc..) Install role Role based install Verify and select machine Select DNS option - include management tools Keep clicking next install DNS manager Tools > DNS Forward lookup zones Make primary zone Enter zone name No dynamic updates finish Validate name Select SOA in FLZ > properties Name server > edit Resolve apply Create host/A record Left click under same area >new host Enter name +ip Add host After this it should be the preferred server Select create associated PTR if RLZ has been made Create a reverse lookup zone Primary zone Select address type Enter IP add(network portion) No dynamic updates Validate name but for RLZ (step 4) Create a pointer record (Ip to name) Right click > new pointer Enter IP Select record from FLZ in hostname Create IPv6 RLZ Primary Select IPv6 Enter network potion + prefix Enter name of file No dynamic updates Go to AAAA record in FLZ and check update PTR recorded Validate SOA

item.title: Commands
item.description: gp update /force rsop  gpresult /? /r /user [user] /verbose

item.title: Installing WAC
item.description: On admin PC Get windows admin center from moodle Go to services Look at remote management Run the installer Accept terms Express setup Gen a self-signed certificate Notify updates Accept default folder Install Take note of the link Click on the link/shortcut In browser when error Advanced Continue at the bottom Login with domain creds Go to admin center Click add Server Active directory Click on server PC On pc Cmd prompt admin winrm quickconfig On serv Active directory Click on server Admin center > settings Install: Active directory DHCP DNS Admin center > server > tools --tool locations

item.title: ADDS Redundancy config
item.description: Preconfig Name Ip Add to domain first Just like the PC Login as domain admin Prepend with domain\admi Add role AD DS Same as before Postconfig promotion Add to existing domain Give password Select which domain controller to pull info from Install Setup DNS Test replication of DNS and AD DS Config failovers
item.guid: ICT 127/Server info/Server roles/Role configurations/ADDS Redundancy config.md

item.title: Symbols info
item.description: --parameters for a cmdlet ` --escape character for str | --pipe "chain" cmdlets together " --double quotes str where PS can look for stuff in it ' --single quote str is a literal str --part of a string is filled in eg: get-service -name s* -- gets services that start with an s .\ --current directory #> -- creates a block comment

item.title: Mar 9 -formatting data
item.description:
formatting data is always the last thing in a string of piped commands
Creating help with a block comment to use as help for get-help when you use '.[Keyword]'
